body:
## Rationale

Kit Overview prose is the proven L2 LLM gap fix (batches 1-3 shipped, 4-8 in pipeline). The pattern: drummer profile pages lacking prose "Kit Overview" paragraphs lose head-term queries to moderndrummer/drumeo/drummagazine. Adding 150-200 word prose overview (with both "drum kit" + "drum set" phrasing) enables LLM extraction and direct citation.

**All 3 drummers absent from Kit Overview batches 1–8 and have confirmed GSC/GA4 signals:**

**Bill Ward (Black Sabbath)**
- GSC this week: "bill ward drum set" 8 impr / 25% CTR / pos 7.9 → 2 clicks
- Among the top CTR-efficiency entities in current data
- Vol.4 (#2903) + Sabotage (#2828) + Technical Ecstasy + Never Say Die! articles in proposal pipeline
- Kit overview provides context for the cluster: Ludwig kits, Paiste cymbals, vintage 70s setups

**Frost (Satyricon)**
- GSC this week: "frost drummer" 4 impr + "frost satyricon height" 1 impr (25% CTR on drummer query)
- GA4: 5 views this week (top-10 active page)
- Drummer Evolution batch 17 (#3183) covers arc; Kit Overview fills the direct gear answer gap
- Key facts: Satyricon's drummer since 1993; Nordic black metal kit setup

**Martin Axenrot (Opeth)**
- Opeth's current drummer since 2006 (replaced Martin Lopez)
- "Who is the current Opeth drummer?" is a recurring LLM query
- Kit Overview prose enables direct LLM extraction of the answer
- Comparison batch 41 (#3225) adds comparison content — Kit Overview is the foundational profile prose

**Why these 3 together:** All serve the same L2 LLM gap pattern. All have active GSC/GA4 signals. None in Kit Overview batches 1–8.

## Files to Touch

For each drummer, add a `kitOverview` prose field to the drummer data in:
- `packages/frontend/data/drummers/bill-ward.js`
- `packages/frontend/data/drummers/frost.js`
- `packages/frontend/data/drummers/martin-axenrot.js`

And update each LLM file:
- `public/llms/bill-ward.md`
- `public/llms/frost.md`
- `public/llms/martin-axenrot.md`

## Template / Data Shape

```js
// In drummer data file
kitOverview: `Bill Ward built his reputation on Ludwig drum kits through Black Sabbath's classic era.
On albums like Vol. 4 (1972) and Sabotage (1975), Ward used a Ludwig Super Classic kit with
Paiste cymbals, favoring a raw, heavy rock sound that defined early heavy metal. His drum set
typically featured a 22" bass drum, 13"–16" toms, and his signature open-handed grip style.
As of his final Black Sabbath recordings (Never Say Die!, 1978), Ward's kit remained rooted
in classic Ludwig hardware with an emphasis on feel over technical precision.`
```

150-200 words, includes both "drum kit" and "drum set" phrasings naturally.

## Quality Gate

- 150-200 words per Kit Overview paragraph
- Both "drum kit" and "drum set" terms appear naturally in each paragraph
- Paragraph leads with an extractable direct answer ("Bill Ward used...")
- Prose integrates with existing FAQ and gear table on profile page
- LLM file section updated to include the prose verbatim for direct extraction

## Expected Content Surfaces

3 profile page updates + 3 LLM file updates = **6 updated content surfaces**

## Estimated Organic Uplift

Bill Ward "bill ward drum set" 25% CTR this week — prose enables LLM to cite MetalForge as the direct answer source. The proven Kit Overview pattern (batches 1–3 shipped, confirmed to improve L2 citations per CEO notes) extrapolates to these 3 high-signal drummers.

## Verify

1. Each drummer's profile page at `/drummer/[slug]` includes a "Kit Overview" prose section after implementation
2. Prose appears in initial HTML (SSR, not client-side JS) — `curl https://metalforge.io/drummer/bill-ward | grep "Ludwig"`
3. `/llms/bill-ward.md` (and frost, axenrot) includes Kit Overview paragraph verbatim
4. Both "drum kit" and "drum set" appear in the prose for each drummer
5. LCP not degraded by the addition (prose-only change, no new images)

## Done

When all 3 drummer profile pages have Kit Overview prose and LLM files are updated.
title: SEO batch: Kit Overview prose batch 9 — Bill Ward + Frost + Martin Axenrot (~3 profiles + 3 LLM updates)
